message: 🌱 Add keylime: keystore password is required when adding compute into mission (#674)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keylime-1135-keystore-password-is-required-when-adding-compute-into-keylime-veri",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keylime: keystore password is required when adding compute into keylime_verifier with keylime_tenant",
+    "description": "keystore password is required when adding compute into keylime_verifier with keylime_tenant. Requested by 0+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current keylime deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your keylime version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime\nhelm list -n keylime 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working keylime installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review keylime configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant keylime configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime\nkubectl get configmap -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keylime\n```\nKeylime CA component requires a password set in its config (ca.conf)"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for keystore password is required when adding compute into",
+        "description": "This is a minimalistic fix for the issue of keystore certificate\npassords. Instead of prompting an user to enter a password during the\nexection of commands such as `keylime_ca --command init` or\n`keylime_tenant -c add ... --cert <cert dir>`, the new code now simply\nreads it from\n```yaml\n# Setup for a user a Keylime credential (only needed one time)\nexport SYSTEMD_CREDENTIAL_SECRET=$HOME/.config/systemd/credential.secret\nsystemd-creds setup\nsystemd-ask-password -n | systemd-creds encrypt - keylime.cred\n\n# I can wrap this in a shell script, or create an alias\nKEYLIME_TENANT_PASSWORD=$(systemd-creds decrypt keylime.cred) keylime_tenant ...\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime\nkubectl get events -n keylime --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"keystore password is required when adding compute into\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This is a minimalistic fix for the issue of keystore certificate\npassords. Instead of prompting an user to enter a password during the\nexection of commands such as `keylime_ca --command init` or\n`keylime_tenant -c add ... --cert <cert dir>`, the new code now simply\nreads it from `/etc/keylime/ca.conf`.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Setup for a user a Keylime credential (only needed one time)\nexport SYSTEMD_CREDENTIAL_SECRET=$HOME/.config/systemd/credential.secret\nsystemd-creds setup\nsystemd-ask-password -n | systemd-creds encrypt - keylime.cred\n\n# I can wrap this in a shell script, or create an alias\nKEYLIME_TENANT_PASSWORD=$(systemd-creds decrypt keylime.cred) keylime_tenant ..."
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keylime",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keylime"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],

+      "issue": "https://github.com/keylime/keylime/issues/1135",

+      "pr": "https://github.com/keylime/keylime/pull/1340"
+    },
+    "reactions": 0,

+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with keylime installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
